prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, ye ask why the sky be so blue, do ye? Well, gather ‘round, ye scurvy dog, and I’ll spin ye a tale as true as Davy Jones’ locker!

‘Tis the work o’ the heavens playin’ tricks with the sun’s golden rays! When Old Sol peeks o’er the horizon, his light be white as a ghost’s shroud, but the sky, she be a clever wench. She takes that light and scatters it every which way, like a kraken tearin’ a merchant ship to splinters!

Now, the blue bits o’ light—them be the nimblest, the quickest, like a cabin boy dartin’ through the riggin’. They bounce and scatter more than the reds and yellows, which be slow as a drunkard on a Sunday morn. So when ye look up, what do ye see? A sea o’ blue, stretched from horizon to horizon, ‘cause them blue rays be winnin’ the race across the sky!

It’s nature’s own magic, lad—no witchcraft, no sorcery, just the way the world be built by the Almighty Navigator Himself! So next time ye gaze aloft, remember: the sky be blue ‘cause the blue light be too swift to catch, and too bold to hide! Now pass the rum, and let’s toast to the blue above!
